evidence_quotes.quote: they are just afraid of getting caught forcing "live service" & "always online" onto it's consumers.
rewriting User License Agreements for games purchased years ago stating now they can shutdown at anytime company feels like it
new updated EULA's even in games that are over 10-15 years old stating that it's a "license" now that they can pull anytime they want.
Revoking the game through a platform an deleting it from ones account so you cannot play or download it ever again without any kind of refunds.

I been playing games for over 30 years and of the more then 3000 games i own digitally more then 64% Has now gotten new EULA's changing it from Purchase to License that can be pulled when Company's want to.
"End Of Life" solution, not one that req. servers to be kept up.
some of them don't even req. internet but still got License Agreements updates stating publisher can pull them whenever for no reason and remove access to them.

paragraphs.text: Greetings. As A Video Games Consumer for more then 30 Years now, I'm terrified of the path the Video Gaming Industry has taken. We need regulations and protections in place to safeguard our ownership of digital purchases. (And to put a stop to Companies re-writing User License Agreements for games purchased years ago stating now they can shutdown at anytime company feels like it). The Gaming Industry want to come in and kill our games, new updated EULA's even in games that are over 10-15 years old stating that it's a "license" now that they can pull anytime they want. Revoking the game through a platform an deleting it from ones account so you cannot play or download it ever again without any kind of refunds. (Ubisoft - The Crew 2014). The Industry will argue that "games will cost more and take longer to produce if they need to build "End Of Life" into there games. Well that's not the case they are just afraid of getting caught forcing "live service" & "always online" onto it's consumers. Games in the past did not need Online Services / Always Online they had options to connect to online functions, having the option to connect means we can still enjoy our memories from that game today. But with this new "always online" we risk losing these video game memories of joy, laugher, our nostalgia. Many of the multiplayer games produced has a fight AI players built in, or explore open world with AI's. These kind of games don't even need online access to function in that form, online part is only used to connecting with other real life players. Those kind of games need safeguards as well when servers are taken down something like pre-build or patch out Online Part. Games cost more now to produce only because of investors greed & the fact companies push out game every year chasing that golden ticket "Live Service" game, they are creating the said same "cost increase" themself by making live service & always online having to keep a server up for years or connection req. to a server for login. that serves no purpose. But to collect user data & information. (The good old games with Direct Connect just write IP Addresses and connect to friends, we can even use today 25 year later so i fail to see the problem as many of said newer games with always online are actually single player games with no need for internet to play but still require it for reasons unknown). Further more to prove a point Pirate Groups that crack game is a lot of the times able to modify these "Always Online" or "Live Service" games into "offline" version that don't connect to Internet, so if these group can do so and release a pirate version just days after org. release. Why can't the Companies themself not do it? (I don't condone Piracy normally, but i think there work bad or good speaks volume against the "It's gonna increase cost & we can't implement offline / end of life it's to difficult). Offline / "Not Always Online" existed before and it needs to again to safeguard digital purchases & ownership. SO we need a "End Of Life" solution, not one that req. servers to be kept up. But a way to keep playing our owned purchases when the time comes and companies pull the plug. I been playing games for over 30 years and of the more then 3000 games i own digitally more then 64% Has now gotten new EULA's changing it from Purchase to License that can be pulled when Company's want to. This was not what i agreed to when i made those purchases back in the days many of them i want to keep playing, some of them don't even req. internet but still got License Agreements updates stating publisher can pull them whenever for no reason and remove access to them. I fear without the StopKillingGames movement & the EU lawmakers taking action, publishers are gonna go unchecked.
